novel_features.name: Delivery comparison board
novel_features.description: See delivery fee, minimum, ETA, rating, and distance for every nearby restaurant side by side, sorted however you want.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join across cached restaurant cards and multi-key client-side sorting that the app buries one tap deep per restaurant.
novel_features.example: grubhub-pp-cli compare "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" --sort eta --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user wants the cheapest or fastest option across the whole neighborhood, not one restaurant at a time.

novel_features.name: Offline dish search
novel_features.description: Find which nearby restaurants carry a specific dish, with price, by full-text searching cached menus across the neighborhood.
novel_features.rationale: Requires SQLite FTS over cached menu items across all nearby restaurants; no single Grubhub API call searches menu item names neighborhood-wide.
novel_features.example: grubhub-pp-cli dish "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" "poke bowl" --max-price 15 --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user names a dish, not a restaurant: 'who near me has a poke bowl under $15'.

novel_features.name: Deal radar
novel_features.description: Rank every nearby restaurant currently running an offer, coupon, or promo code in one sweep.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-restaurant aggregation and value ranking of offers/coupons that the app only shows per-restaurant.
novel_features.example: grubhub-pp-cli deals "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the deal should pick the restaurant, not the other way around.

novel_features.name: Best-value picker
novel_features.description: Get one recommended restaurant from a transparent score over fee, rating, active deals, and ETA.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a deterministic normalized score across cached fee/rating/offer/ETA — no LLM judgment, fully reproducible.
novel_features.example: grubhub-pp-cli pick "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" --weight-deal 2 --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants a single 'just pick one' answer with a visible score breakdown.

narrative.headline: Every Grubhub restaurant search, plus a sortable delivery-fee comparison board, a cross-restaurant deal radar, and offline menu-item search no Grubhub tool has.
narrative.value_prop: grubhub-pp-cli browses Grubhub's marketplace from the command line: search restaurants by street address, browse full menus, and compare delivery fees, minimums, and ETAs across the whole neighborhood at once with `compare`. It caches restaurants and menus in a local SQLite store so `dish` can full-text-search menu items across nearby restaurants and `deals` can rank every active offer in one sweep. No API key required — it mints an anonymous Grubhub token for you.
narrative.auth_narrative: Grubhub has no public API key. grubhub-pp-cli authenticates exactly like the website: it scrapes a fresh anonymous client id from grubhub.com and mints a short-lived bearer token automatically on first use, caching it locally. You don't set anything. For the raw `restaurants` endpoint commands you can optionally run `grubhub-pp-cli auth login` (still credential-free) or set GRUBHUB_TOKEN. Logged-in features like order history are not supported in this version.

quickstart.comment: Confirm Grubhub is reachable before searching.

quickstart.command: grubhub-pp-cli near "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" --sort fee --limit 10
quickstart.comment: Search restaurants near an address, cheapest delivery first.

quickstart.command: grubhub-pp-cli compare "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" --sort eta
quickstart.comment: Side-by-side fee/minimum/ETA board for the whole neighborhood.

quickstart.command: grubhub-pp-cli dish "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" "poke bowl" --max-price 15
quickstart.comment: Find which nearby restaurants carry a dish under a price.

quickstart.command: grubhub-pp-cli deals "350 5th Ave, New York, NY"
quickstart.comment: Rank nearby restaurants by active offers and coupons.

troubleshoots.fix: Grubhub uses PerimeterX bot protection; retry, slow your request rate, or run from a residential network. The CLI already sends a realistic browser User-Agent.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results for a valid address
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm the address geocodes: run `grubhub-pp-cli geocode "<address>"`. If it returns no coordinates, add city/state/zip.
Run `grubhub-pp-cli sync "<address>"` first to cache restaurants and menus for that location.
Tokens are minted automatically; just re-run the command. For the raw `restaurants` surface, re-run `grubhub-pp-cli auth login`.

narrative.when_to_use: Use grubhub-pp-cli when a user wants to find, compare, or order-research food delivery near an address: cheapest or fastest restaurant, a specific dish across the neighborhood, or the best current deal. It is ideal for agents that need deterministic JSON about restaurants, fees, menus, and offers without scraping the Grubhub web app. The local cache makes repeated comparisons over the same location fast and offline.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to place an order or manage a cart — it is read-only marketplace browsing.
Do not use this CLI for order history or account/rewards data — logged-in features require a real Grubhub account login that this version does not support.
Do not use this CLI for DoorDash, Uber Eats, or other delivery services — it only covers Grubhub/Seamless.

recipes.title: Cheapest fast delivery near an address
recipes.command: grubhub-pp-cli compare "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" --sort fee --eta-under 30 --agent
recipes.explanation: Comparison board filtered to sub-30-minute ETAs, cheapest delivery first, as agent JSON.

recipes.title: Find a dish across the neighborhood
recipes.command: grubhub-pp-cli dish "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA" "burrito" --max-price 12
recipes.explanation: Full-text search of cached menus for burritos under $12 at any nearby restaurant.

recipes.title: Pull a full menu as compact JSON for an agent
recipes.explanation: Restaurant menus are deeply nested; --select narrows the payload to item names and prices so agents don't burn context on the full blob.

recipes.title: Let the deal pick the restaurant
recipes.command: grubhub-pp-cli deals "350 5th Ave, New York, NY" --sort value
recipes.explanation: Ranks every nearby restaurant running an offer by value in one sweep.

trigger_phrases: find restaurants near me on grubhub
who near me has a poke bowl
what should I order on grubhub
